key_points: Biodiversity maintains ecological balance and supports life-support systems
Human activities such as deforestation and poaching threaten wildlife
Forests are classified as reserved, protected, and unclassed forests
Community participation plays an important role in forest conservation
Joint Forest Management encourages cooperation between communities and government
examples: Chipko Movement prevented deforestation in the Himalayan region
Sacred groves protect rare plants and animals
Periyar Tiger Reserve protects tigers in Kerala
Villages in Rajasthan protect forests through community rules
formulas: Ecological balance = Interaction among plants, animals, and environment
Forest classification = Reserved forests + Protected forests + Unclassed forests
Community conservation = Participation of local communities in forest protection
exam_tips: Remember the objectives of Project Tiger
Use diagrams or charts when explaining biodiversity
Link conservation concepts with real-life environmental problems
